description: A ketogenic diet adapted for Indian palate. This plan eliminates high-carb staples like rice and roti, replacing them with paneer, eggs, meat, and low-carb vegetables to achieve ketosis.
why_it_works: Ketosis forces your body to burn fat for fuel instead of carbohydrates. By keeping carbs under 30g/day, you deplete glycogen stores within 2-3 days and enter a fat-burning state. The high protein content preserves muscle mass during weight loss.

breakfast.name: Egg omelette with spinach and cheese
Avocado with boiled eggs and butter
Keto dosa (coconut flour) with coconut chutney
Boiled eggs with avocado butter toast (keto bread)

description: A high-protein vegetarian Indian meal plan designed to support muscle growth. Combines paneer, dal, legumes, and dairy to hit protein targets without meat.
why_it_works: Muscle protein synthesis requires adequate leucine intake, achievable through high-volume plant proteins. The strategic combination of incomplete proteins (rice + dal = complete protein) ensures all essential amino acids are present. Caloric surplus supports muscle growth.

breakfast.name: Oats with banana, milk and almonds + protein shake

dinner.name: Soy chunk curry + brown rice + salad

snack.name: Greek yogurt with honey and almonds

dinner.name: Tofu stir fry + brown rice + dal

breakfast.name: Poha with peanuts + protein shake + fruit

lunch.name: Dal makhani + jeera rice + paneer tikka

description: A balanced 1500-calorie Indian diet plan for sustainable weight loss. Includes all traditional Indian foods while maintaining a calorie deficit.
why_it_works: A 500-calorie daily deficit leads to approximately 0.5kg of fat loss per week. This plan maintains metabolic rate by spreading meals across 4 eating occasions and including adequate protein to preserve muscle tissue during the deficit.

lunch.name: Chole (1 bowl) + brown rice (½ cup) + raita
Dal tadka + roti (2) + mixed veg sabzi
Dal makhani (½ bowl) + rice (½ cup) + salad

description: A wholesome vegan Indian meal plan for maintaining weight. Rich in diverse plant proteins, whole grains, and seasonal vegetables.
why_it_works: Plant-based diets tend to be high in fiber which promotes satiety and gut health. This plan achieves protein sufficiency through strategic combination of legumes, seeds, and plant proteins while providing all essential micronutrients.

breakfast.name: Oat porridge with banana, chia seeds, and soy milk

lunch.name: Rajma rice + mixed vegetable sabzi + salad
Pav bhaji (vegan, with soy milk) + salad

breakfast.name: Vegetable upma + flaxseed + soy milk chai

description: An aggressive protein-focused Indian diet plan for body recomposition — losing fat while preserving or building muscle. Prioritizes lean proteins at every meal.
why_it_works: High protein intake (2g per kg of body weight) maximizes muscle protein synthesis while the thermic effect of protein (30% of calories) boosts metabolism. Combined with a moderate caloric deficit, this plan preserves lean mass during fat loss.

Moong dal chilla (3) + curd + whey protein

lunch.name: Soy chunk curry + brown rice + salad

breakfast.name: Poha with soy chunks + whey protein shake

description: A classic ketogenic diet for rapid fat loss. Ultra-low carb (under 30g/day) forces the body into ketosis, burning fat as primary fuel.
why_it_works: Ketosis suppresses appetite through ketone bodies and eliminates insulin spikes. Most people experience rapid initial weight loss from water weight reduction, followed by steady fat loss of 0.5-1kg per week.

description: A plant-based muscle-building diet with no meat. Relies on dairy, eggs, legumes, and plant proteins to meet high protein targets for muscle growth.
why_it_works: Combining dairy (casein + whey), eggs, and legumes ensures complete amino acid profiles. The carbohydrate-rich structure refuels muscle glycogen after training, enabling recovery and growth. Studies show vegetarian athletes can achieve equivalent muscle gains to omnivores with proper planning.

description: The Mediterranean diet is consistently ranked the world's healthiest diet. Rich in olive oil, fish, vegetables, legumes, and whole grains with moderate wine.
why_it_works: The Mediterranean diet reduces LDL cholesterol, inflammation, and cardiovascular risk by 30%. Olive oil's monounsaturated fats and polyphenols protect arteries. Omega-3 rich fish reduce triglycerides. High fiber from vegetables and legumes supports gut health.

description: A high-protein, moderate-carb diet optimized for fat loss without muscle sacrifice. Maintains a 500-calorie deficit while keeping protein at 2g per kg body weight.
why_it_works: Protein has the highest thermic effect of food (TEF) at 20-30%, meaning your body burns more calories digesting protein than carbs or fats. High protein also maximizes satiety, reduces cravings, and preserves muscle during the caloric deficit.

description: A well-balanced, sustainable diet for maintaining healthy weight and overall wellbeing. Follows USDA dietary guidelines with sensible portion control.
why_it_works: Eating at your TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) prevents weight gain while providing all essential nutrients. The 30/40/30 macro split (protein/carbs/fat) is sustainable long-term and supports energy, immunity, and metabolic health.
